title: ophrys insectifera subsp. bertolonii
intro: Ophrys insectifera subsp. bertolonii, also known as the Fly Orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Fly Orchid plant, which is found in the mountainous regions of Italy. The Fly Orchid is a type of orchid that gets its name from its unique ability to mimic the female fly, attracting male flies for pollination. Traditionally, the Fly Orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the Fly Orchid can be made into infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
